question: Does the image display exactly two tabletop celestial demonstration models arranged side by side?
In the left model (solar eclipse), is the Moon positioned between the Sun and the Earth?
In the left model, does a shadow cone project from the Moon onto the Earth?
In the right model (lunar eclipse), is the Earth positioned between the Sun and the Moon?
In the right model, does a shadow cone project from the Earth onto the Moon?
Are the relative sizes of the celestial bodies qualitatively accurate (Sun > Earth > Moon) across both models?
Are the shadow directions in both models consistent with the Sun being the light source (shadows pointing away from the Sun)?
Are the shadow cones depicted with distinguishable umbra (darker central region) and penumbra (lighter outer region) parts?
Is the umbra region correctly depicted as narrower and darker than the surrounding penumbra region?
Do the objects appear to be physical, tabletop-scale demonstration models (e.g., spheres on stands) rather than flat diagrams or digital UI?
Are the celestial bodies and models themselves unlabeled (no text tags, plaques, or captions)?
Is the image free of overlay text, numbers, logos, or watermarks?
Are the celestial bodies (Sun, Earth, Moon) rendered as spherical shapes without significant geometric distortion?
